rulings.Case Title: IN RE: YIN KUON LOW
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Depositions And Production Of Documents And Quash R. Low'S Notice Of Depositions By Oral Examination.
rulings.Rulings: Petitioners, Lena Nagata and Walter Low's Motion to Stay Depositions and Production of Documents and Quash Robyn Low's Notice of Depositions by Oral Examination is hereby GRANTED, in part, as follows: 1) Deposition of Dr. Lee-Tat Chan, MD is hereby STAYED for 45 days. Respondent Robyn Low is granted leave to amend her Deposition Notice to Dr. Chan to limit deposition testimony use to only "[a]t the trial or any other hearing in the action" pursuant to CCP §2025.620. The law does not allow for the use of desposition testimoney for "all purposes." All other grounds for staying Dr. Chan's deposition are hereby overruled. 2) Notices of Deposition for Petitioners/Party Deponents, Lena Negata and Walter Low is hereby QUASHED, without prejudice, for (1) failure to timely serve the Notices of Consumer on consumer, Yin Kuon Low, and (2) improper inclusion of intended use of deposition testimony. Pursuant to CCP §2025.620, deposition testimony may be used only "[a]t the trial or any other hearing in the action" and does not extend to "all purposes." All other grounds for staying or quashing to the Notices of Deposition to Petitioners are hereby overruled. 3) Sanctions shall be AWARDED, in an amount to be determined §§2023.10(i) and 2023.20 Within 15 days of this Order, the Petitioners must provide an accounting of the reasonable expenses they incurred as a result of Respondent and/or her attorney's conduct in this discovery dispute, including attorneys' fees. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on July 9, 2013. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order.
